target_definition: Controlled production means Prophet can run as a secure, deployable, policy-bound defense evidence product with approved customer data, tenant isolation, durable storage, operator identity, real integration handoffs, observability, and a security review packet.

status_scale.done: Implemented, documented, tested, and backed by repo evidence.
status_scale.in_progress: Partially implemented or designed, with remaining acceptance gates open.
status_scale.planned: Accepted into the roadmap, not yet implemented.
status_scale.blocked: Requires customer, legal, security, architecture, or deployment decision before build.

milestones.name: Pilot Release Baseline
acceptance_gates: PR #5 is merged to main at 04fb56b for the internal buyer-pilot baseline; release tagging still waits for release/security gates.
A release tag identifies the exact fixture and smoke hash set.
A second operator can run the evaluator smoke from a fresh clone.

work_items.title: Keep buyer pilot smoke, CI, console, and release-safety gates green
acceptance_gates: Root smoke, Python tests, console lint/build/smoke, dependency audit, and release-safety checks pass.

work_items.title: Tag the merged buyer pilot baseline
acceptance_gates: A tag such as pilot-v0.1 points to the reviewed commit and links to release notes.
work_items.blocker: PR #5 is merged to main for the internal buyer-pilot baseline, but public pilot tagging remains blocked until the historical LOG4SHELL_INSTRUCTIONS.md secret-history finding has an owner decision.

work_items.title: Run fresh-clone smoke on macOS
acceptance_gates: A clean macOS clone completes the 3-minute evaluator path with matching hashes.

work_items.title: Run fresh-clone smoke on Linux
acceptance_gates: A clean Linux clone completes the 3-minute evaluator path with matching hashes.

work_items.title: Publish release notes with expected output hashes
acceptance_gates: Release notes name generated artifact paths, policy hash, evidence hash, and smoke hash manifest.

milestones.name: Production Architecture And Security Design
acceptance_gates: Architecture, threat model, compliance gap map, and execution plan exist.
Customer security reviewers can see data flows, trust boundaries, and remaining gaps without private context.

work_items.title: Create production execution plan
acceptance_gates: The plan defines milestones, acceptance gates, critical path, and non-goals.

work_items.title: Create production architecture decision record
acceptance_gates: The architecture names the first deployable model, services, storage, trust boundaries, and promotion path.

work_items.title: Create STRIDE-style production threat model
acceptance_gates: The threat model covers identity, tenant isolation, data ingestion, evidence, integrations, sandboxing, and operations.

work_items.title: Create compliance and security review gap map
acceptance_gates: The gap map identifies what exists, what is missing, and what blocks CMMC/NIST/FedRAMP/SOC 2 style review.

work_items.title: Create data classification and artifact inventory
acceptance_gates: Every artifact type has classification, allowed fields, retention, storage, and export rules.

milestones.name: Durable Platform Foundation
acceptance_gates: Local stack has an API service, durable database, artifact store abstraction, and background jobs.
Pilot smoke can run through production-shaped services in local mode.

work_items.title: Define API service boundary for runs, policies, evidence, integrations, and readiness
acceptance_gates: API endpoints are versioned, tested, and do not bypass policy checks.

work_items.title: Add Postgres schema and migrations for tenants, policies, runs, artifacts, approvals, and audit events
acceptance_gates: Migrations are deterministic and tests prove tenant-scoped records are required.

work_items.title: Add artifact storage abstraction for evidence bundles and generated handoffs
acceptance_gates: Artifacts are content-addressed, policy-bound, and immutable after approval.

work_items.title: Add job queue abstraction for forecast, sandbox, evidence, and export workflows
acceptance_gates: Long-running workflows have queued job status, retry policy, cancellation, and audit events.

work_items.title: Add production configuration system
acceptance_gates: Local, staging, pilot, and production profiles validate required settings and fail closed.

milestones.name: Identity, RBAC, And Tenant Isolation
acceptance_gates: Users authenticate with customer identity provider or local development identity.
Roles and tenant scope are enforced server-side and covered by negative tests.

work_items.title: Define tenant, organization, workspace, user, role, and service-account model
acceptance_gates: Every persisted object has tenant scope or an explicit global classification.

work_items.title: Implement RBAC roles for viewer, analyst, approver, admin, and auditor
acceptance_gates: Permission tests cover allowed and denied actions for each role.

work_items.title: Add OIDC first, then SAML plan for customers that require it
acceptance_gates: OIDC login maps identity claims to tenant roles without accepting unsigned or missing claims.

work_items.title: Add tenant isolation test harness
acceptance_gates: Automated tests prove tenant A cannot read, modify, export, or infer tenant B artifacts.

work_items.title: Design approval quorum and break-glass controls
acceptance_gates: Higher-risk modes require configured approver count and emit special audit events.

milestones.name: Customer Data And Source Operations
acceptance_gates: Customer-owned inputs can be onboarded through approved schemas.
Source freshness, failure budgets, provenance, and retention are visible and policy-bound.

work_items.title: Add CycloneDX SBOM fixture and parser
acceptance_gates: Safe CycloneDX fixtures produce seedsets without storing unsafe identifiers.

work_items.title: Add SPDX SBOM fixture and parser
acceptance_gates: Safe SPDX fixtures produce seedsets without storing unsafe identifiers.

work_items.title: Implement source failure budgets and fail-closed behavior for required sources
acceptance_gates: Forecast and evidence generation fail closed when required source classes are stale or unavailable.

work_items.title: Implement customer data retention and deletion workflow
acceptance_gates: A tenant admin can request deletion, retention reports identify expired data, and deletion is audited.

work_items.title: Design official-source live collection approval flow
work_items.blocker: Requires legal review, customer-specific policy, retention decision, source terms review, and explicit non-targeting scope.
acceptance_gates: Live collection remains disabled until policy, legal, and source governance gates exist.

milestones.name: Sandbox And Validation Hardening
acceptance_gates: Sandbox validation is reproducible, no-egress, resource-limited, and policy-approved.
Evidence records the artifact provenance chain without raw logs.

work_items.title: Maintain deterministic fixture profiles
acceptance_gates: Edge-appliance and financial-workflow fixture profiles emit valid Direction C artifacts.

work_items.title: Package sandbox runner as a reproducible container
acceptance_gates: Container build is deterministic enough for pilot review and records image digest in run manifest.

work_items.title: Add no-egress and resource-limit enforcement for container mode
acceptance_gates: Tests or documented runtime checks prove no external network, CPU, memory, disk, and timeout limits are configured.

work_items.title: Add sandbox provenance chain from profile to evidence bundle
acceptance_gates: Evidence includes profile ID, artifact hash, run manifest hash, policy hash, and validator result.

work_items.title: Gate every non-fixture sandbox mode on customer approval
work_items.blocker: Requires RBAC, signed approval records, deployment isolation, and customer authorization model.
acceptance_gates: No non-fixture sandbox profile can run without approval record, policy allowlist, and audit event.

milestones.name: Real Integration Workflows
acceptance_gates: Templates can advance to customer-approved dry-run and push modes without bypassing approval.
Secrets are never stored in repo files or evidence outputs.

work_items.title: Add integration configuration schema for Splunk, Elastic, Sentinel, Jira, and ServiceNow
acceptance_gates: Configuration validates placeholders, tenant scope, allowed export kinds, and dry-run mode.

work_items.title: Add secrets manager abstraction for integration credentials
acceptance_gates: Connectors can request credentials by reference only; evidence never contains secret values.

work_items.title: Add dry-run connector mode
acceptance_gates: Connectors validate payload shape against customer sandbox endpoints without changing production systems.

work_items.title: Add approval-required push mode
work_items.blocker: Requires RBAC, signed approvals, customer integration sandbox, rollback plan, and legal/customer authorization.
acceptance_gates: Push mode cannot run without approval quorum, audit event, policy allowlist, and rollback metadata.

work_items.title: Package handoff artifacts as a signed review bundle
acceptance_gates: A single bundle includes manifest, hashes, templates, review checklist, and optional detached signature.

milestones.name: Observability, Deployment, And Operations
acceptance_gates: Production-shaped deployment has health checks, logs, metrics, backups, rollback, and incident procedures.
Operators can diagnose failed workflows without shell access to a developer machine.

work_items.title: Add structured logging contract
acceptance_gates: Logs include tenant, run, policy, action, decision, and correlation IDs without unsafe customer data.

work_items.title: Add metrics and alerting plan
acceptance_gates: Metrics cover job latency, failures, stale sources, policy blocks, export failures, and evidence generation time.

work_items.title: Add health, readiness, and liveness checks
acceptance_gates: Checks distinguish service health, dependency readiness, and policy/evidence blocking failures.

work_items.title: Add deployment and rollback runbooks
acceptance_gates: A release can deploy, verify, rollback, and produce incident notes from written runbooks.

work_items.title: Add backup, restore, and disaster recovery tests
acceptance_gates: Restore drill proves policies, evidence, artifacts, approvals, and audit chains survive backup/restore.

milestones.name: Security Review And Compliance Packet
acceptance_gates: Customer security review packet is complete enough for a paid pilot security team.
Critical and high security-review findings are closed or documented with accepted POA&M.

work_items.title: Build NIST 800-171 and CMMC-oriented security packet
acceptance_gates: Packet includes SSP draft, control matrix, data flows, asset inventory, access controls, IR plan, and POA&M.

work_items.title: Add software supply-chain packet
acceptance_gates: Packet includes dependency inventory, SBOM for Prophet, provenance target, vulnerability process, and update cadence.

work_items.title: Run secrets archaeology and public release review
acceptance_gates: Repository history, current tree, CI logs, and runtime samples are reviewed before public release.
work_items.blocker: Full git-history secret archaeology is blocked on an owner decision for the historical LOG4SHELL_INSTRUCTIONS.md finding recorded in docs/SECRET_HISTORY_REVIEW.md.

work_items.title: Complete incident response playbook
acceptance_gates: Playbook covers data spill, credential exposure, policy bypass, integration misfire, sandbox escape, and customer notification.

work_items.title: Complete external security review
work_items.blocker: Requires production-shaped deployment, threat model, artifact inventory, RBAC, tenant isolation, and security packet.
acceptance_gates: No critical or high findings remain open before production launch.
